message:
You know the scene: your cat climbs onto your lap, settles onto a soft blanket, and starts pressing in with their paws — left, right, left, right — like they're kneading dough. We lovingly call it "making biscuits." But why do they do it? 🐾

It goes all the way back to kittenhood. Nursing kittens knead their mother's belly to help the milk flow, and that motion gets wired in deep as something warm and safe. So when your fully grown cat kneads your lap, they aren't just stretching — they're telling you they feel content, secure, and completely at home with you. Many will purr the whole time, eyes half-closed, drifting toward a nap.

The only downside? Those little claws. A soft blanket between you and the "biscuits" keeps everyone comfy. 💛

Be honest — does your cat knead you, a blanket, or their favorite toy? 😺
